# Agent Log

## Goal
Build a Trello-style Kanban board using a two-agent architecture (Hermes + OpenClaw) through Slack.

## Plan (generated by Hermes)
1. Set up project scaffold and GitHub repo
2. Configure Hermes and OpenClaw agents with Slack
3. Build Laravel backend with REST API and SQLite
4. Build React + Vite frontend
5. Connect frontend to backend API
6. Deploy to Railway (backend) and Vercel (frontend)

## Key Chat Exchanges

### Human → Hermes (terminal)
"plan build a Kanban board with Laravel backend and React frontend"

### Hermes Response
Generated 6-step plan with repo setup, Laravel scaffold, React UI, and deployment steps. Used memory context (GitHub repo name) in the plan.

### Human → OpenClaw (#agent-coder in Slack)
"write a python script that prints Hello from OpenClaw and run it"

### OpenClaw Response
Wrote hello.py, executed it, reported "Hello from OpenClaw!" back in Slack channel.

### Hermes Memory Test (cross-session)
- Session 1: "remember our repo is Project-AI on GitHub, default branch is main"
- Session 2: "recall" → Hermes recalled the fact correctly ✅

### Hermes Status Report Skill
Triggered with "status report" → returned structured:
- What I Did
- What's Left  
- What Needs Your Call

### Hermes Autonomous Cron
Runs every 10 minutes automatically posting to #agent-log without any human prompt ✅

## Progress Reports
- OpenClaw verified working in Slack ✅
- Hermes memory + skill + cron verified ✅
- Laravel API with all endpoints working ✅
- React UI with drag-and-drop working ✅
- Comments feature added ✅
- Email alerts implemented ✅
- Backend deployed on Railway ✅
- Frontend deployed on Vercel ✅

## Human Feedback Given
- Switched Hermes from Gemini to Groq due to rate limits
- Fixed CORS config for production
- Upgraded PHP 8.2 → 8.4 in Dockerfile
- Fixed Apache MPM conflict in Docker
